title: Adrenergic Agonists
summary: الأدرينالية الناهضة (Adrenergic Agonists) هي أدوية تحاكي تأثير الجهاز العصبي الودي عبر تحفيز مستقبلات α و β. تُصنَّف حسب انتقائيتها المستقبلية وآلية عملها (مباشرة أو غير مباشرة). الإبينفرين (Epinephrine) يُحفز α وβ معاً ويُستخدم في الصدمة التأقية وتوقف القلب. الدوبامين والدوبوتامين يُستخدمان في فشل القلب بينما تُستخدم ناهضات β2 الانتقائية كمرخيات قصبية.

key_drugs.name: Epinephrine (Adrenaline)
key_drugs.note: anaphylaxis, cardiac arrest, local anesthetic adjuvant; NOT oral (inactivated by MAO)

key_drugs.name: Norepinephrine (NE)
key_drugs.note: septic shock; IV infusion only; reflex bradycardia due to raised BP

key_drugs.name: Dopamine
key_drugs.note: low dose: vasodilation (D1); intermediate: cardiac (β1); high: vasoconstriction (α1)

key_drugs.name: Dobutamine
key_drugs.note: acute HF with normal BP; more inotropic than chronotropic

key_drugs.name: Isoproterenol
key_drugs.note: rarely used; bradycardia/heart block; replaced by selective β2 agonists for asthma

key_drugs.name: Phenylephrine
key_drugs.note: nasal decongestant; shock; reflex bradycardia (no direct cardiac effect)

key_drugs.name: Oxymetazoline
key_drugs.note: topical nasal decongestant; prolonged use causes rebound congestion

key_drugs.name: Albuterol (Salbutamol)
key_drugs.note: bronchodilator; tocolytic; preferred in acute asthma

key_drugs.name: Salmeterol / Formoterol
key_drugs.note: asthma maintenance; Formoterol faster onset than Salmeterol

mnemonics.title: HAT - آثار الإبينفرين الجانبية
mnemonics.body: H = Headache (throbbing) and cerebral hemorrhage, A = Arrhythmias (tachycardia, palpitation), T = Tolerance

mnemonics.title: D-B-A - الدوبامين حسب الجرعة
mnemonics.body: تخيل دوبامين يبدأ هادئاً (D = Dopamine receptors, توسع أوعية الكلى في الجرعة المنخفضة)، ثم يقوى قلبه (B = Beta1، تحفيز القلب في الجرعة المتوسطة)، ثم يصبح عنيفاً (A = Alpha1، تضيق الأوعية في الجرعة العالية). D ثم B ثم A = تذكر الترتيب الأبجدي

mnemonics.title: Dobutamine vs Dopamine - المقارنة البصرية
mnemonics.body: تخيل طبيباً أمام مريض: إذا الضغط طبيعي والكلى سليمة استخدم Dobutamine (D-o = دو = طبيعي). إذا الضغط منخفض والكلى ضعيفة استخدم Dopamine (D-a = دا = داء = مشكلة). الفرق: Dobutamine للقلب فقط، Dopamine للقلب والكلى والضغط.

mnemonics.title: SAFE - استخدامات الإبينفرين
mnemonics.body: S = Shock (anaphylactic), A = Asthma (acute severe), F = Fibrillation/cardiac arrest, E = Eye (glaucoma, 2% EP) + Epistaxis (bleeding nose)

mnemonics.title: Short vs Long - ناهضات β2
mnemonics.body: الـ SABA (Short-Acting Beta Agonists) مثل Albuterol = للإنقاذ السريع (Rescue). الـ LABA (Long-Acting Beta Agonists) مثل Salmeterol وFormoterol = للصيانة (Maintenance). تذكر: Salmeterol = بطيء البداية، Formoterol = أسرع بداية من Salmeterol.

flashcards.front: Why is Epinephrine not given orally?
flashcards.back: It is inactivated in the gastrointestinal tract and liver by MAO (Monoamine Oxidase); therefore it is given SC, IM, by inhalation, or topically.

flashcards.front: Why does reflex bradycardia occur with Norepinephrine (NE)?
flashcards.back: NE strongly raises arterial blood pressure via α1 → stimulates baroreceptors → activates the vagus nerve → reflex bradycardia that counteracts its weak β1 effect.

flashcards.front: What is the correct sequence of Dopamine effects according to dose?
flashcards.back: Low dose: D1 → renal and mesenteric vasodilation. Intermediate dose: β1 → positive cardiac effect. High dose: α1 → intense vasoconstriction.

flashcards.front: When is Dopamine preferred over Dobutamine in acute heart failure?
flashcards.back: When hypotension is present (α1 raises BP) or when renal function is impaired (D1 improves renal perfusion).

flashcards.front: What are the main adverse effects of Epinephrine (HAT)?
flashcards.back: H = Headache and hemorrhage (elevated BP), A = Arrhythmias (tachycardia and palpitations), T = Tolerance.

flashcards.front: What are the contraindications of Epinephrine?
flashcards.back: 1. Hypertension. 2. Cardiac disease (IHD). 3. Hyperthyroidism (increases β1 receptors and inhibits COMT). 4. Large doses combined with local anesthetic (tissue necrosis).

flashcards.front: Why is Epinephrine used with a local anesthetic?
flashcards.back: It causes local vasoconstriction (α1) → slows absorption of the anesthetic into the bloodstream → prolongs its local duration of action.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between Dobutamine and Dopamine in mechanism of action?
flashcards.back: Dobutamine: selective β1, more positively inotropic than chronotropic. Dopamine: acts on D1 + β1 + α1 progressively according to dose.

flashcards.front: What are the uses of Isoproterenol and is it still widely used?
flashcards.back: Stimulates β1 + β2. Used for bradycardia and heart block (β1). As a bronchodilator (β2), but rarely now because selective β2 agonists are safer.

flashcards.front: What is the effect of Phenylephrine on heart rate and why?
flashcards.back: It causes reflex bradycardia because it is selective for α1 only → raises BP → stimulates baroreceptors → reflex bradycardia via the vagus nerve (not a direct cardiac effect).

flashcards.front: What is the difference between Salmeterol and Formoterol as long-acting β2 agonists?
flashcards.back: Formoterol has a faster onset than Salmeterol (can also be used for rescue). Both are used for maintenance in asthma and COPD.

flashcards.front: Why is Epinephrine given IV in cardiac arrest at a dilution of 1:10,000?
flashcards.back: IV is given only in emergencies (cardiac arrest + CPR). The 1:10,000 dilution reduces the risk of ventricular fibrillation caused by high doses.

flashcards.front: What is the relationship between Hyperthyroidism and Epinephrine?
flashcards.back: T3/T4 upregulates β1 receptors and increases their sensitivity, and inhibits COMT → enhances Epinephrine's cardiac side effects → contraindication.

flashcards.front: What is the use of Epinephrine in glaucoma?
flashcards.back: 2% topical solution in chronic open-angle glaucoma: reduces aqueous humor production via vasoconstriction of ciliary body vessels (α1) → lowers IOP.

flashcards.front: What is Midodrine and why is it used?
flashcards.back: Midodrine: selective α1 agonist taken orally (prodrug). Used in orthostatic hypotension.

flashcards.front: Why is Albuterol preferred over Epinephrine in acute asthma?
flashcards.back: Albuterol is selective for β2 → bronchodilation with less cardiac effect (β1). Epinephrine stimulates α + β1 + β2 together → more cardiac side effects.

flashcards.front: What are the classes of adrenergic agonists by mechanism?
flashcards.back: Direct-acting (Adrenaline, NE, Phenylephrine, Isoproterenol, Dobutamine, Salbutamol), indirect-acting (Amphetamine, Tyramine, Cocaine), and mixed (Ephedrine).

flashcards.front: What is the adrenergic mechanism of Cocaine?
flashcards.back: Blocks reuptake of Norepinephrine → prolongs its action at the synapse.

flashcards.front: What is the effect of an α1 agonist (Phenylephrine)?
flashcards.back: Vasoconstriction → raises blood pressure; nasal decongestant and mydriatic.

flashcards.front: What is the effect of a β1 agonist (Dobutamine)?
flashcards.back: Increases cardiac contractility and rate — used in acute heart failure.

flashcards.front: What is the effect of a β2 agonist (Salbutamol)?
flashcards.back: Bronchodilation — treatment of asthma.

flashcards.front: What are the uses of Adrenaline (Epinephrine)?
flashcards.back: Anaphylaxis, hemostasis, prolongation of local anesthetic effect, cardiac arrest.

flashcards.front: What is the effect of Isoproterenol?
flashcards.back: Non-selective β1 + β2 agonist → tachycardia and bronchodilation.

flashcards.front: Why is Atropine sometimes given with adrenergic agonists?
flashcards.back: To prevent reflex bradycardia resulting from the blood pressure rise (baroreceptor reflex).

flashcards.front: What is the effect of Ephedrine?
flashcards.back: Mixed agonist (direct and indirect) — nasal decongestant and vasopressor with a longer duration of action.

mcqs.q: A patient with septic shock has a blood pressure below 70 mmHg. Which drug is most appropriate?
mcqs.explanation: NE strongly stimulates α1 → potent vasoconstriction → raises BP. Used in septic shock when intense vasoconstriction is needed. Given by IV infusion only.

mcqs.q: A patient with acute heart failure has normal blood pressure and intact renal function. Which drug is most appropriate?
mcqs.explanation: Dobutamine is selective for β1, improves cardiac contractility without significantly affecting heart rate or blood pressure. Preferred when BP is normal and kidneys are intact. Dopamine is used when BP is low or kidneys are impaired.

mcqs.q: A patient with hyperthyroidism needs a local anesthetic for a dental procedure. Can Epinephrine be added?
options: Yes, to prolong the local anesthetic effect
No, because hyperthyroidism is a contraindication to Epinephrine
Yes, but at double the dose
No, because Epinephrine constricts the bronchi
mcqs.explanation: Hyperthyroidism upregulates β1 receptors and increases their sensitivity → Epinephrine may cause dangerous tachycardia, severe hypertension, and risk of myocardial ischemia.

mcqs.q: What is the effect on diastolic blood pressure (diastolic BP) when Epinephrine is given at an intermediate intravenous dose?
options: Falls due to β2 on skeletal muscle vessels
mcqs.explanation: Epinephrine at an intermediate dose stimulates β2 in skeletal muscle vessels → vasodilation → fall in diastolic BP. Systolic BP rises due to β1. The net effect on mean arterial pressure (MAP) depends on dose.

mcqs.q: Why is Norepinephrine (NE) not given SC or IM?
options: Because it is inactivated by oxidation
Because it causes tissue necrosis due to severe vasoconstriction
Because it is well absorbed orally
mcqs.explanation: NE causes intense local vasoconstriction at SC or IM injection sites → severe ischemia → tissue necrosis. Given by IV infusion only.

mcqs.q: Which adrenergic agonist is most appropriate for anaphylactic shock?
mcqs.explanation: Epinephrine is the first-line drug in anaphylactic shock: β2 dilates the bronchi, α1 constricts vessels and raises BP, β1 stimulates the heart. Given IM into the thigh.

mcqs.q: What is the mechanism of action of Albuterol (Salbutamol) in asthma?
options: Blockade of M3 receptors in the bronchi
Stimulation of β2 in bronchial smooth muscle → relaxation
Stimulation of α1 in airway mucosa
mcqs.explanation: Albuterol is a selective β2 agonist → activates adenylyl cyclase → raises intracellular cAMP in smooth muscle cells → bronchial relaxation (bronchodilation).

mcqs.q: A patient takes Salmeterol for asthma maintenance. Can it be used for rapid rescue in an acute attack?
options: Yes, because it is highly effective
No, because its slow onset is unsuitable for emergencies
Yes, but at double the dose
No, because it interacts with other drugs
mcqs.explanation: Salmeterol has a slow onset → not suitable for emergencies. Albuterol (SABA) is used for rapid rescue. Formoterol has a faster onset than Salmeterol, but both are LABAs for maintenance.

mcqs.q: What is the effect of Dopamine on the kidneys at a low dose (2 µg/kg/min)?
options: Renal vasodilation via D1 → increased glomerular filtration
mcqs.explanation: Low-dose Dopamine stimulates D1 receptors in renal and mesenteric vessels → vasodilation → increased renal blood flow and filtration → beneficial in renal failure accompanying heart failure.

mcqs.q: Which of the following drugs is used orally to treat orthostatic hypotension?
mcqs.explanation: Midodrine is a selective α1 agonist taken orally (prodrug converted to its active form). Stimulates vasoconstriction in upright postures → raises BP → beneficial in orthostatic hypotension.

mcqs.q: Cocaine enhances the effect of Norepinephrine by:
mcqs.explanation: Blocks reuptake, prolonging its presence at the synapse.

mcqs.q: Salbutamol is a bronchodilator because it is an agonist at:
The drug of choice in anaphylaxis is:
